verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: sermons on selected lessons of the new testament.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May He, beloved, fulfil your expectation who hath awakened it: for though I feel confident that what I have to say is not my own, but God’s, yet with far more reason do I say, what the Apostle in his humility saith, “We have this treasure in earthen vessels, that the excellency of the power may be of God, and not of us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not doubt accordingly that you remember my promise; in Him I made it through whom I now fulfil it, for both when I made the promise, did I ask of the Lord, and now when I fulfil it, do I receive of Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now you will remember, beloved, that it was in the matins of the festival of the Lord’s Nativity, that I put off the question which I had proposed for resolution, because many came with us to the celebration of the accustomed solemnities of that day to whom the word of God is usually burdensome; but now I imagine that none have come here, but they who desire to hear, and so I am not speaking to hearts that are deaf, and to minds that will disdain the word, but this your longing expectation is a prayer for me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a further consideration; for the day of the public shows has dispersed many from hence, for whose salvation I exhort you to share my great anxiety, and do you with all earnestness of mind, entreat God for those who are not yet intent upon the spectacles of the truth, but are wholly given up to the spectacles of the flesh; for I know and am well assured, that there are now among you those who have this day despised them, and have burst the bonds of their inveterate habits; for men are changed both for the better and the worse.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By daily instances of this kind are we alternately made joyful and sad; we joy over the reformed, are sad over the corrupted; and therefore the Lord doth not say that he who beginneth, shall be saved, “But he that endureth unto the end shall be saved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what more marvellous, what more magnificent thing could our Lord Jesus Christ, the Son of God, and also the Son of man (for this also He vouchsafed to be), grant to us, than the gathering into His fold not only of the spectators of these foolish shows, but even some of the actors in them; for He hath combated unto salvation not only the lovers of the combats of men with beasts, but even the combatants themselves, for He also was made a spectacle Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He hath told us Himself, and foretold it before He was made a spectacle, and in the words of prophecy announced beforehand what was to come to pass, as if it were already done, saying in the Psalms, “They pierced My hands and My feet, they told all My bones.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo! how He was made a spectacle, for His bones to be told! and this spectacle He expresseth more plainly, “they observed and looked upon Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was made a spectacle and an object of derision, made a spectacle by them who were to show Him no favour indeed in that spectacle, but who were to be furious against Him, just as at first He made His martyrs spectacles; as saith the Apostle, “We are made a spectacle unto the world, and to angels, and to men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now two sorts of men are spectators of such spectacles; the one, carnal, the other, spiritual men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The carnal look on, as thinking those martyrs who are thrown to the beasts, or beheaded, or burnt in the flames, to be wretched men, and they detest and abhor them; but others look on, like the holy Angels, not regarding the laceration of their bodies, but admiring the unimpaired purity of their faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A grand spectacle to the eyes of the heart doth a whole mind in a mangled body exhibit!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When these things are read of in the church, you behold them with pleasure with these eyes of the heart, for if you were to behold nothing, you would hear nothing; so you see you have not neglected the spectacles to-day, but have made a choice of spectacles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: May God then be with you, and give you grace with gentle persuasiveness to report your spectacles to your friends, whom you have been pained to see this day running to the amphitheatre, and unwilling to come to the church; that so they too may begin to contemn those things, by the love of which themselves have become contemptible, and may, with you, love God, of whom none who love Him can ever be ashamed, for that they love Him who cannot be overcome: let them, as you do, love Christ, who by that very thing wherein He seemed to be overcome, overcame the whole world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath overcome the whole world as we see, my brethren; He hath subjected all powers, He hath subjugated kings, not with the pride of soldiery, but by the ignominy of the Cross: not by the fury of the sword, but by hanging on the Wood, by suffering in the body, by working in the Spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: His body was lifted up on the Cross, and so He subdued souls to the Cross; and now what jewel in their diadem is more precious than the Cross of Christ on the foreheads of kings?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In loving Him you will never be ashamed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas from the amphitheatre how many return conquered, because those are conquered, for whom they are so madly interested! still more would they be conquered were they to conquer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so would they be enslaved to the vain joy, to the exultation of a depraved desire, who are conquered by the very circumstance of running to these shows.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how many, my brethren, do you think have this day been in hesitation whether they would go here or there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And they who in this hesitation, turning their thoughts to Christ, have run to the church, have overcome, not any man, but the devil himself, him that hunteth after the souls of the whole world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who in that hesitation have chosen rather to run to the amphitheatre, have assuredly been overcome by him whom the others overcame—overcame in Him who saith, “Be of good cheer, I have overcome the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Captain suffered Himself to be tried, only that He might teach His soldiers to fight.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That our Lord Jesus Christ might do this He became the Son of man by being born of a woman.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now, “would He have been any less a man, if He had not been born of the Virgin Mary” one may say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He willed to be a man; well and good; He might have so been, and yet not be born of a woman; for neither did He make the first man whom He made, of a woman.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now see what answer I make to this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You say, Why did He choose to be born of a woman?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I answer, Why should He avoid being born of a woman?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Granted that I could not show that He chose to be born of a woman; do you show why He need have avoided it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But I have already said at other times, that if He had avoided the womb of a woman, it might have betokened, as it were, that He could have contracted defilement from her; but by how much He was in His own substance more incapable of defilement, by so much less had He cause to fear the woman’s womb, as though He could contract defilement from it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But by being born of a woman, He purposed to show to us some high mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For of a truth, brethren, we grant too, that if the Lord had willed to become man without being born of a woman, it were easy to His sovereign Majesty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as He could be born of a woman without a man, so could He also have been born without the woman.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this hath He shown us, that mankind of neither sex might despair of its salvation, for the human sexes are male and female.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If therefore being a man, which it behoved Him assuredly to be, He had not been born of a woman, women might have despaired of themselves, as mindful of their first sin, because by a woman was the first man deceived, and would have thought that they had no hope at all in Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He came therefore as a man to make special choice of that sex, and was born of a woman to console the female sex, as though He would address them and say; “That ye may know that no creature of God is bad, but that unregulated pleasure perverteth it, when in the beginning I made man, I made them male and female.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not condemn the creature which I made.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See I have been born a Man, and born of a woman; it is not then the creature which I made that I condemn, but the sins which I made not.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let each sex then at once see its honour, and confess its iniquity, and let them both hope for salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The poison to deceive man was presented him by woman, through woman let salvation for man’s recovery be presented; so let the woman make amends for the sin by which she deceived the man, by giving birth to Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the same reason again, women were the first who announced to the Apostles the Resurrection of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The woman in Paradise announced death to her husband, and the women in the Church announced salvation to the men; the Apostles were to announce to the nations the Resurrection of Christ, the women announced it to the Apostles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let no one then reproach Christ with His birth of a woman, by which sex the Deliverer could not be defiled, and to which it was in the purpose of the Creator to do honour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, say they, “how are we to believe that Christ was born of a woman?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would answer, by the Gospel which hath been preached and is still preached to all the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But these men, blind themselves, and aiming to blind others, seeing not what they ought to see, whilst they try to shake what ought to be believed, endeavour to obtrude a question on a matter which is now believed through all the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they answer and say: “Do not think to overwhelm us with the authority of the whole world—let us look to Scripture itself, urge not arguments of mere numbers against us, for the seduced multitude favours you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To this I answer, in the first place, “Does the seduced multitude favour me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This multitude was once a scantling.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence grew this multitude, which in this increase was announced so long before?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this which hath been seen to increase, is none other than the same which was seen beforehand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I need not have said, it was a scantling; once it was Abraham only.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider, brethren; it was Abraham alone throughout all the world at that time; throughout the whole world, among all men, and all nations; Abraham alone to whom it was said, “In thy seed shall all nations be blessed;” and what he alone believed of his own single person, is exhibited as present now to many in the multitude of his seed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then it was not seen, and was believed; now it is seen, and it is contested; and what was then said to one man, and was by that one believed, is disputed now by some few, when in many it is made good.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who made His disciples fishers of men, inclosed within His nets every kind of authority.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If great numbers are to be believed, what more widely diffused over the whole world than the Church?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the rich are to be believed, let them consider how many rich He hath taken; if the poor, let them consider the thousands of poor; if nobles, almost all the nobility are within the Church; if kings, let them see all of them subjected to Christ; if the more eloquent, and wise, and learned, let them see how many orators, and scientific men, and philosophers of this world, have been caught by those fishermen, to be drawn from the depth to salvation; let them think of Him who, coming down to heal by the example of His own humility that great evil of man’s soul, pride, “chose the weak things of the world to confound the things which are mighty, and the foolish things of the world to confound the wise” (not the really wise, but who seemed so to be), “and chose the base things of the world, and things which are not, to bring to nought things that are.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Whatever you may choose to say,” they say, “we find that in the place where we read that Christ was born, the Gospels disagree with one another, and two things which disagree cannot both be true;” for, says one, “when I have proved this disagreement, I may rightly disallow belief in it, or, at least, do you who accept the belief in it, shew the agreement.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what disagreement, I ask, will you prove?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A plain one,” says he, “which none can gainsay.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With what security, brethren, do you hear all this, because ye are believers!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend, dearly beloved, and see what wholesome advice the Apostle gives, who says, “As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus our Lord, so walk ye in Him, rooted and built up in Him, and established in the faith;” for with this simple and assured faith ought we to abide stedfastly in Him, that He may Himself open to the faithful what is hidden in Him; for as the same Apostle saith, “In Him are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge;” and He does not hide them to refuse them, but to stir up desire for those hidden things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the advantage of their secrecy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Honour in Him then what as yet thou understandest not, and so much the more as the veils which thou seest are more in number: for the higher in honour any one is, the more veils are suspended in his palace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The veils make that which is kept secret honoured, and to those who honour it, the veils are lifted up; but as for those who mock at the veils, they are driven away from even approaching them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because then we “turn unto Christ, the veil is taken away.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They bring forward then their cavillings, and say, “You allow Matthew is an Evangelist.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We answer: Yes indeed, with a godly confession, and a heart devout, in neither having any doubt at all, we answer plainly, Matthew is an Evangelist.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Do you believe him?” they say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who will not answer, I do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How clear an assent doth that your godly murmur convey!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So, brethren, you believe it in all assurance; you have no cause to blush for it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am speaking to you, who was once deceived, when as in my early boyhood I chose to bring to the divine Scriptures a subtlety of criticising before the godly temper of one who was seeking truth: by my irregular life I shut the gate of my Lord against myself: when I should have knocked for it to be opened, I went on so as to make it more closely shut, for I dared to search in pride for that which none but the humble can discover.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much more blessed now are you, with what sure confidence do you learn, and in what safety, who are still young ones in the nest of faith, and receive the spiritual food; whereas I, wretch that I was, as thinking myself fit to fly, left the nest, and fell down before I flew: but the Lord of mercy raised me up, that I might not be trodden down to death by passers by, and put me in the nest again; for those same things then troubled me, which now in quiet security I am proposing and explaining to you in the Name of the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As then I had begun to say, thus do they cavil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Matthew,” say they, “is an Evangelist, and you believe him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Immediately that we acknowledge him to be an Evangelist, we necessarily believe him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend then to the generations of Christ, which Matthew has set down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The book of the generation of Jesus Christ, the Son of David, the son of Abraham.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How the Son of David, and the Son of Abraham?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He could not be shown to be so, but by the succession of generations; for certain it is that when the Lord was born of the Virgin Mary, neither Abraham nor David was in this world, and dost thou say that the same man is both the Son of David, and the Son of Abraham?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us, as it were, say to Matthew, Prove thy word, for I am waiting for the succession of the generations of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Abraham begat Isaac; and Isaac begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Judas and his brethren; and Judas begat Phares and Zara of Thamar; and Phares begat Esrom; and Esrom begat Aram; and Aram begat Aminadab; and Aminadab begat Naasson; and Naasson begat Salmon; and Salmon begat Booz of Rachab; and Booz begat Obed of Ruth; and Obed begat Jesse; and Jesse begat David the king.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now observe how from this point the genealogy is brought down from David to Christ, who is called the Son of Abraham, and the Son of David.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And David begat Solomon, of her that had been the wife of Urias; and Solomon begat Roboam; and Roboam begat Abia; and Abia begat Asa; and Asa begat Josaphat; and Josaphat begat Joram; and Joram begat Ozias; and Ozias begat Joatham; and Joatham begat Achaz; and Achaz begat Ezekias; and Ezekias begat Manasses; and Manasses begat Amon; and Amon begat Josias; and Josias begat Jechonias and his brethren, about the time they were carried away to Babylon; and after the carrying away into Babylon, Jechonias begat Salathiel; and Salathiel begat Zorobabel; and Zorobabel begat Abiud; and Abiud begat Eliakim; and Eliakim begat Azor; and Azor begat Sadoc; and Sadoc begat Achim; and Achim begat Eliud; and Eliud begat Eleazar; and Eleazar begat Matthan; and Matthan begat Jacob; and Jacob begat Joseph the husband of Mary, of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus then by the order and succession of fathers and forefathers, Christ is found to be the Son of David, and the Son of Abraham.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now upon this thus faithfully narrated, the first cavil they bring is, that the same Matthew goes on to say, “All the generations from Abraham to David are fourteen generations; and from David until the carrying away into Babylon are fourteen generations; and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ are fourteen generations.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then in order to tell us how Christ was born of the Virgin Mary, he went on and said, “Now the birth of Jesus Christ was on this wise;” for by the line of the generations he had showed why Christ is called the Son of David, and the Son of Abraham.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now it needed to be shown how He was born and appeared among men: and so there follows immediately that narrative, by means of which we believe that our Lord Jesus Christ was not only born of the everlasting God, coeternal with Him who begat Him before all times, before all creation, by whom all things were made; but was also now born from the Holy Ghost, of the Virgin Mary, which we confess equally with the other; for you remember and know (for I am speaking to Catholics, to my brethren), that this is our faith, that this we profess and confess; for this faith thousands of martyrs have been slain in all the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This also which follows they like to laugh at, whose wish it is to destroy the authority of the Evangelical books, that they may show as it were that we have without any good reason believed what is said, “When as His mother Mary was espoused to Joseph, before they came together, she was found with Child of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then Joseph her husband being a just man, and not willing to make her a public example, was minded to put her away privily;” for because he knew that she was not with child by him, he thought that she was so to say necessarily an adulteress.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Being a just man,” as the Scripture saith, “and not willing to make her a public example,” (that is, to divulge the matter, for so it is in many copies), “he was minded to put her away privily.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The husband indeed was in trouble, but as being a just man he deals not severely; for so great justice is ascribed to this man, as that he neither wished to keep an adulterous wife, nor could bring himself to punish and expose her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He was minded to put her away privily,” because he was not only unwilling to punish, but even to betray her; and mark his genuine justice; for he did not wish to spare her, because he had a desire to keep her; for many spare their adulterous wives through a carnal love, choosing to keep them even though adulterous, that they may enjoy them through a carnal desire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this just man has no wish to keep her, and so does not love in any carnal sort; and yet he does not wish to punish her; and so in his mercy he spares her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How truly just a man is this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would neither keep an adulteress, lest he should seem to spare her because of an impure affection, and yet he would not punish or betray her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Deservedly indeed was he chosen for the witness of his wife’s virginity: and so he who was in trouble through human infirmity, was assured by Divine authority.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Evangelist goes on to say, “While he thought on these things, behold, the angel of the Lord appeared unto him in sleep, saying, Joseph, fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife; for That which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And she shall bring forth a Son, and thou shalt call His name Jesus.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why Jesus? “for He shall save His people from their sins.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is well known then, that “Jesus” in the Hebrew tongue is in Latin interpreted “Saviour,” which we see from this very explanation of the name; for as if it had been asked, “Why Jesus?” he subjoined immediately as explaining the reason of the word, “for He shall save His people from their sins.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then we religiously believe, this most firmly hold fast, that Christ was born by the Holy Ghost of the Virgin Mary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then do our adversaries say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If,” says one, “I shall discover a lie, surely you will not then believe it all; and such I have discovered.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us see: I will reckon up the generations; for by their slanderous cavillings they invite and bring us to this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, if we live religiously, if we believe Christ, if we do not desire to fly out of the nest before the time, they only bring us to this—to the knowledge of mysteries.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark then, holy brethren, the usefulness of heretics; their usefulness, that is, in respect of the designs of God, who makes a good use even of those that are bad; whereas, as regards themselves, the fruit of their own designs is rendered to them, and not that good which God brings out of them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Just as in the case of Judas; what great good did he!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the Lord’s Passion all nations are saved; but that the Lord might suffer, Judas betrayed Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God then both delivers the nations by the Passion of His Son, and punishes Judas for his own wickedness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the mysteries which lie hid in Scripture, no one who is content with the simplicity of the faith would curiously sift them, and therefore as no one would sift them, no one would discover them but for cavillers who force us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when heretics cavil, the little ones are disturbed; when disturbed, they make search, and their search is, so to say, a beating of the head at the mother’s breasts, that they may yield as much milk as is sufficient for these little ones.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They search then, because they are troubled; but they who know and have learnt these things, because they have investigated them, and God hath opened to their knocking, they in their turn open to those who are in trouble.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so it happens that heretics serve usefully for the discovery of the truth, whilst they cavil to seduce men into error.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For with less carefulness would truth be sought out, if it had not lying adversaries; “For there must be also heresies among you,” and as though we should enquire the cause, he immediately subjoined, “that they which are approved may be made manifest among you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is it that they say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “See; Matthew enumerates the generations, and says, that “from Abraham to David are fourteen generations, and from David until the carrying away into Babylon are fourteen generations, and from the carrying away into Babylon unto Christ are fourteen generations.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now three times fourteen make forty-two; yet they number them, and find them forty-one generations, and immediately they bring up their cavilling and their insulting mockery, and say, “What means it, when in the Gospel it is said that there are three times fourteen generations, yet when they are numbered all together, they are found to be not forty-two, but forty-one?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doubtless there is a great mystery here: and glad are we, and we give thanks unto the Lord, that by the occasion of cavillers we have discovered something which gives us in the discovery the more pleasure, in proportion to its obscurity when it was the object of search; for, as I have said before, we are exhibiting a spectacle to your minds.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: From Abraham then to David are fourteen generations: after that, the enumeration begins with Solomon, for David begat Solomon; the enumeration, I say, begins with Solomon, and reaches to Jechonias, during whose life the carrying away into Babylon took place; and so are there other fourteen generations, by reckoning in Solomon at the head of the second division, and Jechonias also, with whom that enumeration closes to fill up the number fourteen; and the third division begins with this same Jechonias.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give attention, holy brethren, to this circumstance, at once mysterious and pleasant; for I confess to you the feeling of my own heart, whereby I believe that when I have brought it forth, and you have got taste of it, you will give the same report of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend then.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the third division, beginning from this Jechonias unto the Lord Jesus Christ, are found fourteen generations; for this Jechonias is reckoned twice, as the last of the former, and the first of the following division.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But why is Jechonias,” one may say, “reckoned twice?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing took place of old among the people of Israel, which was not a mysterious figure of things to come: and indeed it is not without good reason that Jechonias is reckoned twice, because if there be a boundary between two fields, be it a stone, or any dividing wall, both he who is on the one side measures up to that same wall, and he who is on the other takes the beginning of his measurement again from the same.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why this was not done in the first connecting link of the divisions, when we number from Abraham to David fourteen generations, and begin to reckon the fourteen others, not from David over again, but from Solomon, a reason must be given which contains an important mystery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend then.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The carrying away into Babylon took place when Jechonias was appointed king in the room of his deceased father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The kingdom was taken from him, and another appointed in his room; still the carrying away unto the Gentiles took place during the lifetime of Jechonias, for no fault of Jechonias is mentioned for which he was deprived of the kingdom; but the sins rather of those who succeeded him are marked out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then there follows the Captivity and the passing away into Babylon; and the wicked do not go alone, but the saints also go with them: for in that Captivity were the prophets Ezekiel and Daniel, and the Three Children who were cast into the flames, and so made famous.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They all went according to the prophecy of the prophet Jeremiah.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Remember then, that Jechonias, rejected without any fault of his, ceased to reign, and passed over unto the Gentiles, when the carrying away unto Babylon took place.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now observe the figure hereby manifested beforehand, of things to come in the Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Jews would not that our Lord Jesus Christ should reign over them, yet found they no fault in Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was rejected in His own person, and in that of His servants also, and so they passed over unto the Gentiles as into Babylon in a figure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this also did Jeremiah prophesy, that the Lord commanded them to go into Babylon: and whatever other prophets told the people not to go into Babylon, them he reproved as false prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let those who read the Scriptures, remember this as we do; and let those who do not, give us credit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Jeremiah then on the part of God threatened those who would not go into Babylon, whereas to them who should go he promised rest there, and a sort of happiness in the cultivation of their vines, and planting of their gardens, and the abundance of their fruits.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then does the people of Israel, not now in figure but in verity, pass over unto Babylon?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence came the Apostles?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Were they not of the nation of the Jews?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence came Paul himself? for he saith, “I also am an Israelite, of the seed of Abraham, of the tribe of Benjamin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many of the Jews then believed in the Lord; from them were the Apostles chosen; of them were the more than five hundred brethren, to whom it was vouchsafed to see the Lord after His resurrection; of them were the hundred and twenty in the house, when the Holy Ghost came down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what saith the Apostle in the Acts of the Apostles, when the Jews refused the word of truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We were sent unto you, but seeing ye have rejected the word of God, lo! we turn unto the Gentiles.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The true passing over then into Babylon, which was then prefigured in the time of Jeremiah, took place in the spiritual dispensation of the time of the Lord’s Incarnation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what saith Jeremiah of these Babylonians, to those who were passing over to them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For in their peace shall be your peace.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When Israel then passed over also into Babylon by Christ and the Apostles, that is, when the Gospel came unto the Gentiles, what saith the Apostle, as though by the mouth of Jeremiah of old?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I exhort therefore, that, first of all, supplications, prayers, intercessions, and giving of thanks be made for all men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For kings, and for all that are in authority; that we may lead a quiet and peaceable life in all godliness and honesty.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they were not yet Christian kings, yet he prayed for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Israel then praying in Babylon hath been heard; the prayers of the Church have been heard, and the kings have become Christian, and you see now fulfilled what was then spoken in figure; “In their peace shall be your peace,” for they have received the peace of Christ, and have left off to persecute Christians, that now in the secure quiet of peace, the Churches might be built up, and peoples planted in the garden of God, and that all nations might bring forth fruit in faith, and hope, and love, which is in Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The carrying away into Babylon took place of old by Jechonias, who was not permitted to reign in the nation of the Jews, as a type of Christ, whom the Jews would not have reign over them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Israel passed over unto the Gentiles, that is, the preachers of the Gospel passed over unto the people of the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What marvel then, that Jechonias is reckoned twice? for if he were a figure of Christ passing over from the Jews unto the Gentiles, consider only what Christ is between the Jews and Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is He not that Corner-stone?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In a corner-stone you see the end of one wall, and the beginning of another; up to that stone you measure one wall, and another from it; therefore the corner-stone which connects both walls is reckoned twice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Jechonias then as prefiguring the Lord was, as it were, a type of the corner-stone; and as Jechonias was not permitted to reign over the Jews, but they went unto Babylon, so Christ, “the stone which the builders rejected, is made the head of the corner,” that the Gospel might reach unto the Gentiles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hesitate not then to reckon the head of the corner twice, and you have at once the number written: and so there are fourteen in each of the three divisions, yet altogether the generations are not forty-two, but forty-one; for as when the order of the stones runs in a straight line, they are all reckoned but once, but when there is a deviation from the straight line to make an angle, that stone at which the deviation begins must be reckoned twice, because it belongs at once to that line which is finished at it, and to that other line which begins from it; so as long as the order of the generations continued in the Jewish people, it made no angle in the regular division of fourteen; but when the line was turned that the people might pass over into Babylon, a sort of angle as it were was made at Jechonias, so that it was necessary to reckon him twice, as the type of that adorable Corner-stone.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They have another cavil.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The generations of Christ,” say they, “are numbered through Joseph, and not through Mary.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend awhile, holy brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “It ought not to be,” they say, “through Joseph.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was not Joseph the husband of Mary?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “No,” they say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who says so?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Scripture saith by the authority of the Angel that he was her husband.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Fear not to take unto thee Mary thy wife, for That which is conceived in her is of the Holy Ghost.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, he was commanded to name the Child, though He was not born of his seed; “She shall bring forth a Son, and thou shalt call His name Jesus.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now the Scripture is intent on showing, that He was not born of Joseph’s seed, when he is told in his trouble as to her being with child, “He is of the Holy Ghost;” and yet his paternal authority is not taken from him, forasmuch as he is commanded to name the Child; and again the Virgin Mary herself, who was well aware that it was not by him that she conceived Christ, yet calls him the father of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider when this was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Lord Jesus, as to His Human Nature, was twelve years old (for as to His Divine Nature He is before all times, and without time), He tarried behind them in the temple, and disputed with the elders, and they wondered at His doctrine; and His parents who were returning from Jerusalem sought Him among their company, among those, that is, who were journeying with them, and when they found Him not, they returned in trouble to Jerusalem, and found Him disputing in the temple with the elders, when He was, as I said, twelve years old.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what wonder?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word of God is never silent, though it is not always heard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is found then in the temple, and His mother saith to Him, “Why hast Thou thus dealt with us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy father and I have sought Thee sorrowing;” and He said, “Wist ye not that I must be about My Father’s service?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This He said for that the Son of God was in the temple of God, for that temple was not Joseph’s, but God’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See, says some one, “He did not allow that He was the Son of Joseph.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wait, brethren, with a little patience, because of the press of time, that it may be long enough for what I have to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When Mary had said, “Thy father and I have sought Thee sorrowing,” He answered, “Wist ye not that I must be about My Father’s service?” for He would not be their Son in such a sense, as not to be understood to be also the Son of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Son of God He was—ever the Son of God—Creator even of themselves who spake to Him; but the Son of Man in time; born of a Virgin without the operation of her husband, yet the Son of both parents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence prove we this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Already have we proved it by the words of Mary, “Thy father and I have sought Thee sorrowing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now in the first place for the instruction of the women, our sisters, such saintly modesty of the Virgin Mary must not be passed over, brethren.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She had given birth to Christ—the Angel had come to her, and said, “Behold, thou shall conceive in thy womb, and bring forth a Son, and shalt call His name Jesus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He shall be great, and shall be called the Son of the Highest.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She had been thought worthy to give birth to the Son of the Highest, yet was she most humble; nor did she put herself before her husband, even in the order of naming him, so as to say, “I and Thy father,” but she saith, “Thy father and I.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: She regarded not the high honour of her womb, but the order of wedlock did she regard, for Christ the humble would not have taught His mother to be proud.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy father and I have sought Thee sorrowing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thy father and I, she saith, “for the husband is the head of the woman.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much less then ought other women to be proud! for Mary herself also is called a woman, not from the loss of virginity, but by a form of expression peculiar to her country; for of the Lord Jesus the Apostle also said, “made of a woman,” yet there is no interruption hence to the order and connection of our Creed wherein we confess “that He was born of the Holy Ghost and the Virgin Mary.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as a virgin she conceived Him, as a virgin brought Him forth, and a virgin she continued; but all females they called “women,” by a peculiarity of the Hebrew tongue.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear a most plain example of this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first woman whom God made, having taken her out of the side of a man, was called a woman before she “knew” her husband, which we are told was not till after they went out of Paradise, for the Scripture saith, “He made her a woman.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The answer then of the Lord Jesus Christ, “I must be about My Father’s service,” does not in such sense declare God to be His Father, as to deny that Joseph was His father also; And whence prove we this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the Scripture, which saith on this wise, “And He said unto them, Wist ye not that I must be about My Father’s service; but they understood not what He spake to them: and when He went down with them, He came to Nazareth, and was subject to them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It did not say, “He was subject to His mother,” or was “subject to her,” but “He was subject to them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To whom was He subject? was it not to His parents?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was to both His parents that He was subject, by the same condescension by which He was the Son of Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: A little way back women received their precepts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now let children receive theirs—to obey their parents, and to be subject to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The world was subject unto Christ, and Christ was subject to His parents.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You see then, brethren, that He did not say, “I must needs be about My Father’s service,” in any such sense as that we should understand Him thereby to have said, “You are not My parents.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were His parents in time, God was His Father eternally.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were the parents of the Son of Man—“He,” the Father of His Word, and Wisdom, and Power, by whom He made all things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if all things were made by that Wisdom, “which reacheth from one end to another mightily, and sweetly ordereth all things,” then were they also made by the Son of God to whom He Himself as Son of Man was afterwards to be subject; and the Apostle says that He is the Son of David, “who was made of the seed of David according to the flesh.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But yet the Lord Himself proposes a question to the Jews, which the Apostle solves in these very words; for when he said, “who was made of the seed of David,” he added, “according to the flesh,” that it might be understood that He is not the Son of David according to His Divinity, but that the Son of God is David’s Lord; for thus in another place, when He is setting forth the privileges of the Jewish people, the Apostle saith, “Whose are the fathers, of whom as concerning the flesh Christ came, Who is over all, God blessed for ever.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As, “according to the flesh,” He is David’s Son; but as being “God over all, blessed for ever,” He is David’s Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord then saith to the Jews, “Whose Son say ye that Christ is?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They answered, “The Son of David.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this they knew, as they had learnt it easily from the preaching of the Prophets; and in truth, He was of the seed of David, “but according to the flesh,” by the Virgin Mary, who was espoused to Joseph.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When they answered then that Christ was David’s Son, Jesus said to them, “How then doth David in spirit call Him Lord, saying, The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on My right hand, till I put Thine enemies under Thy feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If David then in spirit call Him Lord, how is He his Son?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Jews could not answer Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So we have it in the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not deny that He was David’s Son, so that they could not understand that He was also David’s Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For they acknowledged in Christ that which He became in time, but they did not understand in Him what He was in all eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore wishing to teach them His Divinity, He proposed a question touching His Humanity; as though He would say, “You know that Christ is David’s Son, answer Me, how He is also David’s Lord?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that they might not say, “He is not David’s Lord,” He introduced the testimony of David himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what doth he say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saith indeed the truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For you find God in the Psalms saying to David, “Of the fruit of thy body will I set upon thy seat.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here then He is the Son of David.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how is He the Lord of David, who is David’s Son?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The Lord said unto my Lord, Sit Thou on My right hand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Can you wonder that David’s Son is his Lord, when you see that Mary was the mother of her Lord?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is David’s Lord then as being God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: David’s Lord, as being Lord of all; and David’s Son, as being the Son of Man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At once Lord and Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: David’s Lord, “who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God;” and David’s Son, in that “He emptied Himself, taking the form of a servant.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Joseph then was not the less His father, because he knew not the mother of our Lord, as though concupiscence and not conjugal affection constitutes the marriage bond.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend, holy brethren; Christ’s Apostle was some time after this to say in the Church, “It remaineth that they that have wives be as though they had none.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And we know many of our brethren bringing forth fruit through grace, who for the Name of Christ practise an entire restraint by mutual consent, who yet suffer no restraint of true conjugal affection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, the more the former is repressed, the more is the other strengthened and confirmed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are they then not married people who thus live, not requiring from each other any carnal gratification, or exacting the satisfaction of any bodily desire?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet the wife is subject to the husband, because it is fitting that she should be, and so much the more in subjection is she, in proportion to her greater chastity; and the husband for his part loveth his wife truly, as it is written, “In honour and sanctification,” as a coheir of grace: as “Christ,” saith the Apostle, “loved the Church.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then this be a union, and a marriage; if it be not the less a marriage because nothing of that kind passes between them, which even with unmarried persons may take place, but then unlawfully; (O that all could live so, but many have not the power!) let them at least not separate those who have the power, and deny that the man is a husband or the woman a wife, because there is no fleshly intercourse, but only the union of hearts between them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hence, my brethren, understand the sense of Scripture concerning those our ancient fathers, whose sole design in their marriage was to have children by their wives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For those even who, according to the custom of their time and nation, had a plurality of wives, lived in such chastity with them, as not to approach their bed, but for the cause I have mentioned, thus treating them indeed with honour.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But he who exceeds the limits which this rule prescribes for the fulfilment of this end of marriage, acts contrary to the very contract by which he took his wife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The contract is read, read in the presence of all the attesting witnesses; and an express clause is there that they marry “for the procreation of children;” and this is called the marriage contract.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If it was not for this that wives were given and taken to wife, what father could without blushing give up his daughter to the lust of any man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now, that the parents may not blush, and that they may give their daughters in honourable marriage, not to shame, the contract is read out.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is read from it?—the clause, “for the sake of the procreation of children.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when this is heard, the brow of the parent is cleared up and calmed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us consider again the feelings of the husband who takes his wife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The husband himself would blush to receive her with any other view, if the father would blush with any other view to give her.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, if they cannot contain (as I have said on other occasions), let them require what is due, and let them not go to any others than those from whom it is due.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let both the woman and the man seek relief for their infirmity in themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let not the husband go to any other woman, nor the woman to any other man, for from this adultery gets its name, as though it were “a going to another.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if they exceed the bounds of the marriage contract, let them not at least exceed those of conjugal fidelity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it not a sin in married persons to exact from one another more than this design of the “procreation of children” renders necessary?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is doubtless a sin, though a venial one.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle saith, “But I speak this of allowance,” when he was treating the matter thus.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Defraud ye not one the other, except it be with consent for a time, that ye may give yourselves to fasting and prayer; and come together again, that Satan tempt you not for your incontinency.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What does this mean?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That you do not impose upon yourselves any thing beyond your strength, that you do not by your mutual continence fall into adultery.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That Satan tempt you not for your incontinency.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that he might not seem to enjoin what he only allowed (for it is one thing to give precepts to strength of virtue, and another to make allowance to infirmity), he immediately subjoined; “But this I speak of allowance, not of commandment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I would that all men were even as I myself.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As though he would say, I do not command you to do this; but I pardon you if you do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, my brethren, give heed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those famous men who marry wives only for the procreation of children, such as we read the Patriarchs to have been, and know it, by many proofs, by the clear and unequivocal testimony of the sacred books; whoever, I say, they are who marry wives for this purpose only, if the means could be given them of having children without intercourse with their wives, would they not with joy unspeakable embrace so great a blessing? would they not with great delight accept it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For there are two carnal operations by which mankind is preserved, to both of which the wise and holy descend as matter of duty, but the unwise rush headlong into them through lust; and these are very different things.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now what are these two things by which mankind is preserved?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The first which is confined to ourselves and relates to taking nourishment (which cannot of course be taken without some gratification of the flesh), is eating and drinking; if you do not this you will die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By this one support then of eating and drinking does the race of man subsist, by a law of its nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But by this men are only supported as far as themselves are concerned; for they do not provide for any succession by eating and drinking, but by marrying wives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so is the race of man preserved; first, by the means of life; but because whatever care they exercise they cannot of course live for ever, there is a second provision made, that those who are newly born may replace those who die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the race of man is, as it is written, like the leaves on a tree, or an olive, that is, or a laurel, or some tree of this sort, which is never without foliage, yet whose leaves are not always the same.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, as it is written, “it shooteth forth some, and casteth others,” because those which sprout afresh replace the others as they fall, for the tree is ever casting its leaves, yet is ever clothed with leaves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So also the race of man feels not the loss of those who die day by day, because of the supply of those who are newly born; and thus the whole race of mankind is according to its own laws sustained, and as leaves are ever seen on the trees, so is the earth seen to be full of men.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas if they were only to die, and no fresh ones be born, the earth would be stripped of all its inhabitants, as certain trees are of all their leaves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seeing then that the human race subsists in such sort, as that those two supports, of which enough has now been said, are necessary to it, the wise, and understanding, and the faithful man descends to both as matter of duty, and does not fall into them through lust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But how many are there who rush greedily to their eating and drinking, and make their whole life to consist in them, as if they were the very reason for living.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For whereas men really eat to live, they think that they live to eat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These will every wise man condemn, and holy Scripture especially, all gluttons, drunkards, gormandizers, “whose god is their belly.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nothing but the lust of the flesh, and not the need of refreshment, carries them to the table.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These then fall upon their meat and drink.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who descend to them from the duty of maintaining life, do not live to eat, but eat to live.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly, if the offer were made to these wise and temperate persons that they should live without food or drink, with what great joy would they embrace the boon! that now they might not even be forced to descend to that into which it had never been their custom to fall, but that they might be lifted up always in the Lord, and no necessity of repairing the wastings of their body might make them lay aside their fixed attention towards Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How think ye that the holy Elias received the cruse of water, and the cake of bread, to satisfy him for forty days?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With great joy no doubt, because he eat and drank to live, and not to serve his lust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But try to bring this about, if you could, for a man who, like the beast in his stall, places his whole blessedness and happiness in the table.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would hate your boon, and thrust it from him, and look upon it as a punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so in that other duty of marriage, sensual men seek for wives only to satisfy their sensuality, and therefore at length are scarce contented even with their wives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I would that if they cannot or will not cure their sensuality, they would not suffer it to go beyond that limit which conjugal duty prescribes, I mean even that which is granted to infirmity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Nevertheless, if you were to say to such a man, “why do you marry?” he would answer perhaps for very shame, “for the sake of children.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if any one in whom he could have unhesitating credit were to say to him, “God is able to give, and yea, and will give you children without your having any intercourse with your wife;” he would assuredly be driven to confess that it was not for the sake of children that he was seeking for a wife.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him then acknowledge his infirmity, and so receive that which he pretended to receive only as matter of duty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was thus those holy men of former times, those men of God sought and wished for children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this one end—the procreation of children, was their intercourse and union with their wives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is for this reason that they were allowed to have a plurality of wives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if immoderateness in these desires could be well-pleasing to God, it would have been as much allowed at that time for one woman to have many husbands, as one husband many wives.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then had all chaste women no more than one husband, but one man had many wives, except that for one man to have many wives is a means to the multiplication of a family, whereas a woman would not give birth to more children, how many soever more husbands she might have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore, brethren, if our fathers’ union and intercourse with their wives, was for no other end but the procreation of children, it had been great matter of joy to them, if they could have had children without that intercourse, since for the sake of having them they descended to that intercourse only through duty, and did not rush into it through lust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then was Joseph not a father because he had gotten a son without any lust of the flesh?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God forbid that Christian chastity should entertain a thought, which even Jewish chastity entertained not!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Love your wives then, but love them chastely.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In your intercourse with them keep yourselves within the bounds necessary for the procreation of children.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And inasmuch as you cannot otherwise have them, descend to it with regret.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this necessity is the punishment of that Adam from whom we are sprung.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us not make a pride of our punishment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is his punishment who because he was made mortal by sin, was condemned to bring forth only a mortal posterity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This punishment God has not withdrawn, that man might remember from what state he is called away, and to what state he is called, and might seek for that union, in which there can be no corruption.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Among that people then, because it was necessary that there should be an abundant increase until Christ came, by the multiplication of that people in whom were to be prefigured all that was to be prefigured as instruction for the Church, it was a duty to marry wives, by means of whom that people in whom the Church should be foreshown might increase.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the King of all nations Himself was born, then began the honour of virginity with the mother of the Lord, who had the privilege of bearing a Son without any loss of her virgin purity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As that then was a true marriage, and a marriage free from all corruption, so why should not the husband chastely receive what his wife had chastely brought forth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For as she was a wife in chastity, so was he in chastity a husband; and as she was in chastity a mother, so was he in chastity a father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whoso then says that he ought not to be called father, because he did not beget his Son in the usual way, looks rather to the satisfaction of passion in the procreation of children, and not the natural feeling of affection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What others desire to fulfil in the flesh, he in a more excellent way fulfilled in the spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thus they who adopt children, beget them by the heart in greater chastity, whom they cannot by the flesh beget.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider, brethren, the laws of adoption; how a man comes to be the son of another, of whom he was not born, so that the choice of the person who adopts has more right in him than the nature of him who begets him has.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not only then must Joseph be a father, but in a most excellent manner a father.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For men beget children of women also who are not their wives, and they are called natural children, and the children of the lawful marriage are placed above them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now as to the manner of their birth, they are born alike; why then are the latter set above the other, but because the love of a wife, of whom children are born, is the more pure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The union of the sexes is not regarded in this case, for this is the same in both women.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where has the wife the pre-eminence but in her fidelity, her wedded love, her more true and pure affection?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then a man could have children by his wife without this intercourse, should he not have so much the more joy thereby, in proportion to the greater chastity of her whom he loves the most?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See too by this how it may happen, that one man may have not two sons only, but two fathers also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For by the mention of adoption, it may occur to your thoughts that so it may be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is said; A man can have two sons, but two fathers he cannot have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the truth is, it is found that he can have two fathers also, if one have begotten him of his body, and another adopted him in love.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If one man then can have two fathers, Joseph could have two fathers also; might be begotten by one, and adopted by another.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And if this be so, what do their cavillings mean, who insist that Matthew has followed one set of generations, and Luke another?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And in fact we find that so it is, for Matthew has given Jacob as the father of Joseph, and Luke Heli.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it is true it might seem, as if one and the same man, whose son Joseph was, had two names.
